anchoring_evidence: BG 2.14 transitions from the sāṅkhya block's substrate-doctrine into the operator-stability discipline that will become the sthita-prajña block. The engineering layer correctly anchors on both: titikṣā is the discipline that flows from the Self-knowledge of 2.11-2.13. Tags ['observability', 'metrics-discipline', 'alerting-discipline', 'operator-system-coupling'] are at the steady-mind / operator-stance edge of the chapter.
hard_edge_preservation: PASS — the chapter's hard edge that 'non-attachment ≠ not caring' is preserved. The engineering layer is precise: 'they are not detached from feedback; the verse is precise that contacts are genuinely informative.' Falsifiability rules out the 'ignore the dashboard' misreading.
counter_anti_thesis_check: PASS — the verse does not detach engineers from users (the scenario explicitly involves customer impact and routing the work appropriately), does not absent consequence-weight (signals are read, not denied), does not flatten svadharma into arbitrary work.
progressive_build: PASS — at position 14, the verse correctly transitions from the substrate-doctrine of 2.11-2.13 toward the operator-stance teaching that 2.15 will cap. Pedagogical position correct. The voice is appropriate: the doctrine has been established (substrate is real), and now the operator's relationship to incoming sensation is being taught.
